name: Muntanya de les Creus Trail
name_original: Subida a la montaña de Les Creus por les Fontetes — circular desde Tavernes de la Valldigna
description: Hike the moderate 5.8-mile Ascent to Les Creus circular trail from Tavernes de la Valldigna for panoramic views and historic paths.
region_intro: Hiking around Tavernes De La Valldigna offers diverse landscapes, situated between the Iberian and Betic mountain systems and the Mediterranean coast in the La Safor region of Valencia. The area features prominent mountain ranges like the Mondúver massif, providing varied terrains and panoramic views. Extensive orange groves add a unique charm to the lower elevations, while natural springs like El Clot de la Font offer refreshing stops. This geographical blend creates a network of trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Tavernes De La Valldigna The most popular hiking route is Muntanya de les Creus Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and historic paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the Marinyen castle via the Clot spring — circular route through the Mondúver massif, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This route combines historical exploration with views of the Mondúver massif. Local hikers also love the Senda de la Cadira and Penyal de Ferragud Loop, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Tavernes De La Valldigna is defined by mountainous backdrops, Mediterranean coastal views, and charming orange groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more demanding routes. The routes in Tavernes De La Valldigna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Tavernes De La Valldigna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tavernes De La Valldigna?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes around Tavernes De La Valldigna, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Tavernes De La Valldigna offers 10 easy routes perfect for beginners or families. These trails typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a relaxed outdoor experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Tavernes De La Valldigna?
A: The region boasts a diverse landscape, from the dramatic backdrops of the Iberian and Betic mountain systems to the serene Mediterranean coast. You'll encounter extensive orange groves, natural springs like El Clot de la Font, and panoramic viewpoints from peaks such as the Mondúver massif. Trails often combine mountainous terrain with coastal views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Tavernes De La Valldigna are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374310431/ascent-to-the-marinyen-castle-via-the-clot-spring-circular-route-through-the-monduver-massif" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Marinyen castle via the Clot spring — circular route through the Mondúver massif&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.3 km loop. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376101016/from-tavernes-de-la-valldigna-to-the-monastery-simat-de-la-valldigna-circular-through-the-orange-fields" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tavernes de la Valldigna to the monastery Simat de la Valldigna — circular through the orange fields&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a cultural stop.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on a hike?
A: You can combine hiking with historical exploration. The area features sites like the Tavernes Castle (Castillo de Alcalà de Alfàndec) and the remains of defense walls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374310431/ascent-to-the-marinyen-castle-via-the-clot-spring-circular-route-through-the-monduver-massif" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Marinyen castle via the Clot spring&lt;/a&gt; leads to Marinyen Castle. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376101016/from-tavernes-de-la-valldigna-to-the-monastery-simat-de-la-valldigna-circular-through-the-orange-fields" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route through orange fields&lt;/a&gt; can take you to the Monastery of Santa María de la Valldigna.

Q: Are there any caves or natural monuments to visit near the trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its caves and natural features. You can explore significant caves like the Cova del Bolomor, which holds ancient archaeological remains. Other notable caves include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3297906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Galera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Verge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4758759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Municipal Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tavernes De La Valldigna?
A: The routes in Tavernes De La Valldigna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views from mountain peaks, and the charming passages through orange groves.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tavernes De La Valldigna?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Tavernes De La Valldigna. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially for more strenuous routes, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this period.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are 7 difficult routes available. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374305083/senda-de-la-cadira-and-penyal-de-ferragud-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Cadira and Penyal de Ferragud Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 16 km trail with significant elevation gain, often taking over 5 hours to complete and requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Can I find information about natural springs or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Many trails pass by natural springs and offer stunning viewpoints. El Clot de la Font is a notable natural spring located at the foot of the mountains, providing a refreshing stop. The Mondúver massif is considered one of the best viewpoints in the province, offering panoramic vistas. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7083728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cullera from the Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find more information about the natural areas of Tavernes De La Valldigna?
A: For more details on the natural landscapes and environmental aspects of the region, you can visit the official tourism website. Check out &lt;a href="https://www.visitatavernes.es/parajes-naturales/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitatavernes.es/parajes-naturales/&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.visitatavernes.es/en/environmental-tavernes/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitatavernes.es/en/environmental-tavernes/&lt;/a&gt; for additional context.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The route usually takes about 3 hours and 50 minutes to complete. However, some variants can extend the duration up to 5 hours, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to the mountain of Les Creus by les Fontetes — circular from Tavernes de la Valldigna suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate hike, requiring good physical fitness and sure-footedness due to ascents and descents. While not for absolute beginners, those with some hiking experience should manage it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly accessible paths but also includes demanding sections. You'll encounter a steep initial ascent, rocky mountain areas, and the historic "Senda dels Amoladors" on the descent, which is a zigzagging path with large stones and a "vertiginous descent."

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular panoramic views of the Valldigna valley, the coast, and the sea from various natural viewpoints. Key points include the Les Creus Summit Cross, the El Calvari Defensive Wall and Canal, and the Fontetes de Cantus. On clear days, you might even spot Ibiza or the Montgó massif.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Ascent to the mountain of Les Creus by les Fontetes — circular from Tavernes de la Valldigna?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route from Tavernes de la Valldigna, and the starting point by Les Fontetes is a common access point. Specific parking information is not detailed, but Tavernes de la Valldigna offers various parking options within the town, from where you can access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or steep sections. Ensure your dog is also fit enough for a moderate hike with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the ascents more challenging, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and potentially more unpredictable weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ascent to the mountain of Les Creus by les Fontetes — circular from Tavernes de la Valldigna. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana and Carrer Castellets.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes by the Fontetes de Cantus, a small spring. Additionally, the Font de la Sangonera is mentioned as a natural spring that often includes a pleasant recreational area. However, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a fully charged phone. Trekking poles can be helpful for the steep ascents and descents, especially on the Senda dels Amoladors.

waypoints.name: El Calvari Defensive Wall and Canal
